text: What is a predicate in logic?
answer: A statement whose truth value depends on variables
b: A statement whose truth value depends on variables
c: A proposition that is always true
d: A proposition that is always false

text: Which symbol represents the universal quantifier?
answer: \(\forall\)

text: To prove \(\forall x P(x)\) is true, one must show:
answer: \(P(x)\) is true for every \(x\) in the domain
c: \(P(x)\) is true for every \(x\) in the domain
d: \(P(x)\) is false for every \(x\) in the domain

text: An example that makes \(\exists x P(x)\) true is called a/an _____.
answer: example

text: What shows that a universally quantified statement is false?
answer: Providing a counterexample
a: Showing it holds for an arbitrary element

text: The statement \(\exists x P(x)\) is true if:
answer: \(P(x)\) is true for at least one \(x\)
b: \(P(x)\) is true for at least one \(x\)
d: \(P(x)\) is false for at least one \(x\)

text: If the domain is empty, the statement \(\forall x P(x)\) is:
answer: True

text: Which expression is a proposition?
answer: \(P(3)\) where \(3\) is a constant

text: What distinguishes a predicate from a proposition?
answer: A predicate contains variables and lacks a definite truth value until variables are specified.
a: A predicate contains variables and lacks a definite truth value until variables are specified.
b: A predicate always has a true truth value.
d: A predicate is a compound proposition.

text: The universal quantifier \(\forall x P(x)\) means:
answer: \(P(x)\) is true for every \(x\) in the domain.
a: There exists some \(x\) such that \(P(x)\) is false.
b: \(P(x)\) is true for at least one \(x\) in the domain.
c: \(P(x)\) is true for every \(x\) in the domain.
d: \(P(x)\) is false for all \(x\) in the domain.

text: What is required to prove the statement \(\exists x P(x)\) is true?
answer: Provide an example with one \(x\) where \(P(x)\) is true.
a: Show \(P(x)\) is true for every \(x\) in the domain.
b: Give a counterexample where \(P(x)\) is false.
c: Provide an example with one \(x\) where \(P(x)\) is true.

text: How can a universally quantified statement \(\forall x P(x)\) be shown false?
answer: By giving a counterexample where \(P(x)\) is false.
a: By finding an example where \(P(x)\) is true.
b: By giving a counterexample where \(P(x)\) is false.
c: By proving \(P(x)\) is true for an arbitrary \(x\).
d: By showing \(P(x)\) is true for all \(x\).

text: Fill in the blank: The domain of a variable in a predicate is the set of all possible _____ for the variable.
answer: values

text: Why is the statement \(P(5)\) called a proposition if \(P(x)\) is a predicate?
answer: Because \(P(5)\) has a well-defined truth value.
c: Because \(P(5)\) has a well-defined truth value.

text: Fill in the blank: To show \(\forall x P(x)\) is true, one can prove \(P(x)\) holds for an _____ element \(x\).
answer: arbitrary

text: What is the truth value of \(\exists x (x + 1 < x)\) for positive integers \(x\)?
answer: False, because no positive integer satisfies \(x + 1 < x\).
b: False, because no positive integer satisfies \(x + 1 < x\).
d: False, because the domain is empty.

text: What is a predicate in logic?
answer: A logical statement with a truth value depending on variables
b: A statement with a fixed truth value
c: A logical statement with a truth value depending on variables

text: Which symbol denotes a universal quantifier?
answer: \(\forall\)

text: To prove \(\forall x P(x)\) is true, what must be shown?
answer: \(P(x)\) is true for every \(x\) in the domain
b: \(P(x)\) is true for every \(x\) in the domain

text: A single counterexample to \(\forall x P(x)\) does what?
answer: Shows the statement is false

text: If the domain is empty, what is the truth value of \(\forall x P(x)\)?
answer: True

text: The statement \(\exists x P(x)\) means:
answer: \(P(x)\) is true for at least one \(x\)
b: \(P(x)\) is true for at least one \(x\)

text: To show \(\exists x P(x)\) is false, what must be done?
answer: Show \(P(x)\) is false for all \(x\)

text: Is \(P(5)\) (where \(P(x): x\) is odd) a proposition or predicate?
answer: It is a proposition with a truth value
b: It is a proposition with a truth value

text: A logical statement with a truth value depending on variables is called a/an _____.
answer: predicate

text: The symbol \(\forall\) in \(\forall x P(x)\) represents a/an _____ quantifier.
answer: universal

text: To show \(\forall x P(x)\) is true, one must prove _____.
answer: \(P(n)\) is true for every \(n\) in the domain
a: \(P(x)\) is false for at least one \(x\)
b: \(P(n)\) is true for every \(n\) in the domain
c: \(P(n)\) is true for some \(n\) in the domain

text: A counterexample for \(\forall x P(x)\) is _____.
answer: an element where \(P(x)\) is false

text: The statement \(\exists x P(x)\) means _____.
answer: \(P(x)\) is true for at least one \(x\)
c: \(P(x)\) is true for at least one \(x\)

text: To prove \(\exists x P(x)\) is true, it suffices to _____.
answer: find a single example where \(P(x)\) is true
b: find a single example where \(P(x)\) is true
d: prove \(P(x)\) is true for the empty domain

text: The domain of a variable in a predicate is _____.
answer: the set of all possible values for the variable
a: the truth value of the predicate
b: the set of all possible values for the variable
c: the specific value assigned to the variable

text: The statement "\(x\) is an odd number" is a predicate because _____.
answer: it contains a variable and depends on \(x\)
a: it has a well-defined truth value without specifying \(x\)
b: it contains a variable and depends on \(x\)
c: it is true for all integers
d: it is false for all integers
